summary: Backend testing of v3.6.14 Redfish/BMC Auto-Discovery feature. Created /app/backend/tests/test_bmc_auto_discovery.py with 22 tests covering: connector network-discovery payload with bmc_candidates persistence, db.bmc_candidates upsert (idempotency verified, no duplicates on re-POST), discovered_endpoints enrichment with bmc_kind/bmc_version, JWT-protected admin endpoints (list/dismiss/import), import->device creation with redfish_enabled=True/created_via=bmc_auto_discovery, re-import already_exists path, _guess_endpoint_type unit tests for all 5 BMC kinds (ilo/idrac/ipmi/xcc/redfish_generic) with priority over hostname/ports, plus legacy payload regression. All 22/22 tests pass.

minor.issue: Line 2686-2688: db.bmc_candidates.delete_many({client_id, ip: {$in: managed_ip_set_for_bmc}}) is OK but redundant — the upsert at 2666-2684 already skips IPs in managed_ip_set_for_bmc. The delete_many also has no time-based pruning despite the comment 'Drop BMC candidates not seen in last 24h or already managed' (24h logic is missing).

discovered_endpoints enrichment query does not filter by client_id. If two clients have a BMC at the same IP, switch_ip/port could leak across clients. Recommend adding client_id filter to the find().

Creates record in db.devices, but the rest of the platform mainly uses db.managed_devices for polling. The created device may not be auto-polled until the admin re-creates it as a managed device. Worth documenting the follow-up step (vault credentials + managed_devices link).

action_items: Optional: enrich GET /api/bmc-candidates query with client_id filter on discovered_endpoints to avoid cross-tenant leakage (low risk in practice since IPs are private but still a defense-in-depth fix).
Optional: implement the 24h pruning for db.bmc_candidates referenced in the comment but not actually coded.
Optional: create a corresponding managed_devices entry on import (or document the follow-up vault step in the UI).
critical_code_review_comments: connector.py is now 3022 lines — well over the 700-line guideline. Should be split into submodules (heartbeat/devices/discovery/bmc/maintenance) for maintainability.
BMC import endpoint writes to db.devices but most of the codebase uses db.managed_devices: dual-write pattern needs to be reconciled.
GET /api/bmc-candidates enrichment ignores client_id when querying discovered_endpoints — minor cross-client risk.
test_credentials: admin@86bit.it / password (JWT). Test client created on the fly via POST /api/clients.
seed_data_creation: Per test: 1 client TEST_bmc_<uuid>, 1 device_poll_status (switch 10.88.88.1), 3 BMC candidates (10.88.88.50/51/52 - ilo/idrac/ipmi). All cleaned up in fixture teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: v3.6.14 BMC auto-discovery is fully covered by /app/backend/tests/test_bmc_auto_discovery.py. The test file is order-sensitive within TestNetworkDiscoveryBMCPersistence (idempotency test re-posts full payload to keep state). Module-scoped fixture creates a fresh client every run. Useful for regression on /api/connector/network-discovery, /api/bmc-candidates*, and routes.topology._guess_endpoint_type.
